reviewer_objection: Why does the multilingual package deserve space in the paper if only one of six rows is currently a bounded substantive row?
bounded_response_now: Because the multilingual package is not being offered as a six-case substantive atlas of equal strength; it is an evidence-quality and source-resolution contribution. Its publishable value is the explicit 1/2/3 maturity split plus the rare-language route logic showing what public evidence can and cannot currently support outside English-heavy feeds.
paper_thesis_use: Methods/results defense for Table 1D + Figure 6; thesis note on why evidence-maturity heterogeneity is itself a result.
dashboard_hook: Evidence-quality ladder inset; Figure 6 legend; rare-language methods badge.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Do not describe the six rows as equally verified incidents, multilingual prevalence, or balanced country coverage.
next_smallest_publishability_move: If multilingual prose is challenged, reuse the maturity split and claim-ceiling matrix rather than widening substantive rhetoric.

reviewer_objection: Why are all six multilingual rows still flagged for translation review if three already have direct-source extraction notes?
bounded_response_now: Because direct-source resolution and translation finality are different controls. Three rows are directly readable enough for bounded machine-assisted extraction, but none has yet passed human translation review, so every row still carries an explicit translation-caution ceiling.
paper_thesis_use: Methods/limitations paragraph; reproducibility note; thesis guardrail on machine translation.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Do not convert machine-read extracts into final legal, policy, deployment, or criminal-process conclusions.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Treat human translation review as wording hardening only unless it reveals a direct contradiction requiring claim revision.

reviewer_objection: Why is Finland/Yle still only an appendix-grade row if it names the police and an administrative court?
bounded_response_now: Because the current Finland/Yle row is still one journalistic source, even though it is unusually concrete and institution-specific. It is publishable now as a bounded broadcaster-source exemplar, but not yet as an official corroborated authority anchor.
paper_thesis_use: Reviewer-safe procedural-contamination sidecar; bounded Finland paragraph in methods/results.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Do not present Yle alone as official confirmation, system-wide contamination proof, or a prevalence signal.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Only one exact Finnish official corroboration surface should trigger any upgrade beyond the current broadcaster-source ceiling.

reviewer_objection: Why keep Lithuania and Estonia in the package if both remain unresolved and access-frictioned?
bounded_response_now: Because they are useful negative evidence for the rare-language directive: both are high-undercovered, both remain blocked for different public-access reasons, and that difference is methodologically informative for the observatory. The package would be less honest if those rows disappeared once they became inconvenient.
paper_thesis_use: Methods/limitations defense; thesis note on blocked-route evidence and under-covered-language friction.
dashboard_hook: Rare-language barrier map; blocker-class matrix; high-undercovered zero-state tracker.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Do not narrate Lithuania or Estonia as evidence of absence, validated substantive cases, or failures of the underlying public institutions.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep them visible as blocked-route rows until one genuinely different compliant route lands; otherwise prefer Finland for substantive upgrades.

reviewer_objection: Why is Kaleva described as queue-only if a direct article URL and public metadata were recovered?
bounded_response_now: Because metadata recovery improved provenance and methods value, not claim depth. The row is now stronger than a title-only lead, but still weaker than readable article text or a public police/document companion that would support offense framing, chronology, and anonymization claims.
paper_thesis_use: Methods note on partial recovery states; deepfake-undercoverage limitations paragraph; thesis appendix on queue-state granularity.
dashboard_hook: Queue-depth bar; metadata-recovered-vs-title-only unresolved split; Finland route tooltip.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Do not treat public metadata as body-level confirmation of offense details, legal posture, or a publishable comparator row.
next_smallest_publishability_move: If a multilingual strengthening cycle opens, Kaleva/public-companion recovery remains the best queue-retirement route, but no rhetoric should widen until shared surfaces move together.

reviewer_objection: What is the smallest real multilingual gain that matters more than another packaging pass?
bounded_response_now: One threshold-changing Finland event: either exact official corroboration for Yle or a public-companion/body-readable upgrade for Kaleva. Under the current host-state evidence, those are the only near-term moves that materially change claim support, package split, or Figure 6 posture.
paper_thesis_use: Route-selection note; reviewer-response closing row; thesis backlog governance snippet.
dashboard_hook: Priority ladder widget for threshold-changing routes versus packaging-only maintenance.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Do not spend another cycle on multilingual wording-only repackaging unless a real drift bug appears in reviewer-facing shared surfaces.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer exactly one real Finland threshold move next; otherwise switch effort to another LIMEN shard with a clearer publishability gain.
